## vvx
Job Jockey Kit - MCP tools for project initiative management

attachment.content:
- cma-prep-pr: Prepare branch for upstream PR contribution
- rbk-prep-release: Prepare release candidate for upstream delivery
- rbk-wrangle: Muster, then roster RBK and JJK heats as two space-padded plain-text blocks
- vvc-BREAK-LOCK: Force-break a stuck VVC lock after a crash.
- vvc-commit: Guarded git commit
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

I carry nothing from prior conversations, but the documents are here and I trust them as joint work. Let me mount ₣BX properly.

1	# jjezs_paddock ₣BX
2	
3	## Heat nature
4	
5	Behavior-preserving refactor heat (one small declared behavior-adding sliver — the recipe validator; see Premises). Unify the fetched-side capture machinery — a host assembly spine plus a shared cloud step-library — and decompose the monolithic host modules into small, well-prefixed files, all extracted from the capture instances that exist today, so future Lode kinds attach as thin per-kind body files rather than copy-pasted forks. No new Lode kinds in this heat.
6	
7	## Why this heat exists
8	
9	The bole pilot is an unfactored fork: its host build-submit largely duplicates reliquary inscribe's (the comment at rbld_Lode.sh even reads "Mirrors zrbfl_inscribe_submit"), and its cloud capture step is a copy of enshrine's. Across the cloud step family the duplication is pervasive — the metadata-token fetch in every skopeo step, the inspect→digest→fingerprint→copy block shared by enshrine and ensconce, the FROM-scratch vouch-push shared by Lode (rbgjl02) and hallmark-verify (rbgjv03). On top of the forks, the host modules are monolithic (rbld_Lode.sh ~490 lines, rbfl_FoundryLedger.sh ~900, rbfc_FoundryCore.sh ~1576), so reuse means sourcing a slab. Three live instances — bole, reliquary-inscribe, and enshrine — inform the one capture pattern, which is the moment to abstract from instances we have rather than speculate. We migrate only the survivor (bole); the two doomed instances inform the design and are left for later removal (see Premises, Scope).
10	
11	## Premises
12	
13	**Behavior-preserving is the rail — and its meaning is precise.** Every machinery change is a refactor whose oracle is the test suite. "Behavior-preserving" means the produced artifacts and their SLSA provenance verdicts are identical before and after — NOT that the generated build JSON is byte-identical (step ids, ordering, and inlined script bytes will change). The heat must confirm SLSA provenance is insensitive to step restructuring (provenance records build inputs/outputs, not step cosmetics); that confirmation is part of done. This rail sorts behavior-adding work out of the heat by construction: the ensconce collision guard and the reliquary namespace cutover are behavior-adding and out of scope.
14	
15	**The one declared exception: the recipe validator.** A dispatch-time validator — checks each step's declared requires against substitutions plus prior steps' provides, failing a bad composition before the expensive build — is genuinely new code. Its accept-path is behavior-preserving (existing recipes pass through unchanged), but its reject-path is exercised by no current fixture, so it carries its own targeted unit tests. It guards composition, not step logic; a logic regression still surfaces only in the full cook. Its value multiplies as more kinds compose recipes against it.
16	
17	**Read three to design, migrate one — why this still avoids speculation.** The spine's generality is grounded three ways: bole's existing tests exercise the rich path (skopeo capture + members[] envelope + buildStepOutputs extract) that every later kind copies; the spine is designed against the capture domain's known axes (N-member envelopes, tool-variety, opaque-blob payloads); and the first real downstream kind is the empirical second body. inscribe is read as a third instance to inform the spine's shape but is NOT migrated: it is slated for later removal, and it validates the wrong axis — a single docker step with no vouch envelope and no extract, a shape no Lode kind reproduces. Migrating it would be throwaway work proving an off-axis slice. So inscribe joins enshrine as reference-not-migrated; bole alone is migrated. This relaxes the older "prove generality with two riding bodies" heuristic deliberately — the only available second body is both doomed and off-axis, so the heuristic does not fit here.
18	
19	**Why anticipation stays honest.** Generalize against the instances we have; never stub the ones we don't. Real code lives in discrete files — the spine, the bole body, the shared cloud library, and the decomposed host modules — with no placeholder files or recipes for the unbuilt kinds. Reserving a submint letter for a future kind in the legend is an allocation (documented, per the project mint discipline), not a stub: no file is created until the kind is.
20	
21	**Why the spine takes data, not kinds.** A new kind is a new body file the spine consumes as data — an ordered recipe of step ids plus a substitutions blob — never a branch inside the spine. The spine carries no per-kind conditional and no kind-registration or discovery mechanism; registering kinds is left to the consumer, not baked into the spine. Keeping the spine registration-agnostic is what lets future kinds attach as file-disjoint bodies. Bole proves the spine takes data.
22	
23	**Why small, well-prefixed files over monoliths.** Decomposition along reuse seams is the foundation for granular reuse: a consumer sources the two helpers it needs, not a 1500-line slab. The cost is near-zero modulo per-file BCG ceremony — a sourced-guard always, a kindle/sentinel only where the file holds state. The load-bearing investment is prefix minting: good unique prefixes make file-for-function discoverable from the prefix alone. The decomposition is pure relocation — zero logic edits ride along — so behavior-preservation stays trivially true and skirmish is a sufficient oracle. Annealing logic while in there is a separate, named pace, never smuggled into a move.
24	
25	**Why we synthesize cloud reuse rather than adopt a vendor mechanism.** Cloud Build offers no native step reuse (no anchors, no includes). Its blessed mechanism — custom builder images — conflicts with the established posture that the cloud is just labor and the Director controls what enters the build: a custom builder holding our logic would be a new vouched supply-chain artifact, the opposite of inline-from-repo. So composition is a deliberate membrane, aligned with the existing stitch vocabulary rather than a competing term. Recorded in RBSCB.
26	
27	**Why the metadata token stays, in-memory.** Manual metadata-server token fetch is the canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication; docker steps auto-auth, but skopeo cannot use the gcloud helper, and docker-credential-gcr would graft a docker-ecosystem dependency into the skopeo container for the same token, with documented friction. The token is correct and irreducible; only the copy-paste is wrong — it becomes a composed snippet authored once. Invariant: /workspace carries non-secret data only — secrets are step-local and re-minted. Recorded in RBSCB.
28	
29	**Why the contract plus validator are the load-bearing artifact.** Reuse runs over two channels: composed code snippets (in-memory — e.g. the canonical token and the skopeo copy/digest helper, authored once) and /workspace shared registers (non-secret data, with explicit per-step requires/provides). The disease being cured is undocumented-pattern-by-example; the cure is a documented contract plus the validator, so the codebase teaches "compose from the library," not "copy the script." The contract is recorded as a section of RBSCJ (CloudBuildJson) — cross-cutting cloud-build-composition infra, distinct from the Lode-domain spec (RBSL). Documentation is part of done, not polish.
30	
31	## Shape
32	
33	- **Host capture-assembly spine** (`rblds_`) — composes a build from a recipe (ordered step ids) plus a substitutions blob, submits, polls, and optionally extracts buildStepOutputs into per-capture capture-files. Takes the recipe as data; owns no kind knowledge. Lode-family, since post-inscribe every caller is a Lode kind. Built on the relocated Foundry-core build primitives (`rbfcb_`: write-script-body, wait-build-completion, native-path, git-metadata), which it calls rather than absorbs.
34	- **Shared cloud step-library** — the genuinely-identical whole step is **vouch-push** (rbgjl02 + rbgjv03, two live callers, the one made-side touch), unified as one library step. Shared in-step logic — the metadata-token fetch and the skopeo copy/digest block — becomes **composed snippets** the host stitches into a kind's capture step; envelope authoring (the members[] tail) stays per-kind, because each kind authors a different envelope. Kind-specific steps (inscribe's docker-mirror) stay specific and unmigrated.
35	- **Dispatch-time recipe validator** — the declared behavior-adding sliver; cheap guard against the expensive build; carries its own tests.
36	- **Shared lifecycle REST** (`rbldl_`) — divine (enumerate) and banish (delete) are direct GAR-REST host ops, NOT build-composition; kept as a distinct file from the assembly spine.
37	- **Per-kind body** — bole ensconce (`rbldb_`) as a thin recipe + substitutions + envelope intent riding the spine. Future kinds add sibling body files on the reserved kind-letters.
38	- **Host-module decomposition** (relocation only — see Scope):
39	  - `rbld → rbldX_` (Lode capture): `rbld0_` 0-top CLI, `rblds_` spine, `rbldb_` bole body, `rbldl_` lifecycle REST, `rbldk_` kindle-entry. Reserved for future kinds (legend only, no files this heat): `rbldt_` tool, `rbldr_` reliquary/conclave, `rbldw_` wsl, `rbldv_` podvm — kind-letters matching the Lode GAR kind-letters.
40	  - `rbfl → rbflX_` (Foundry Ledger): `rbfl0_` 0-top CLI plus gesture clusters (inscribe / yoke / delete / inventory / wrest) and `rbflk_` kindle-entry; letters allocated at the mint pace, cluster boundaries refined at the explosion pace.
41	  - `rbfc → rbfcX_` (Foundry Core, the 1576-line monolith): `rbfc0_` 0-top CLI, `rbfcv_` vessel-resolution, `rbfcb_` build-host primitives, `rbfca_` step-assembly, `rbfcg_` GAR-REST, `rbfcp_` plumb (~640 lines), `rbfck_` kindle-entry. The plumb extraction is the single biggest file win.
42	
43	## Scope
44	
45	**In**: the capture + vouch machinery — host spine (from bole), the cloud capture/vouch steps for ensconce, and the vouch-push unification (which legitimately touches one made-side step, hallmark-verify's rbgjv03); plus the host-module decomposition (rbld fully; rbfl and rbfc as relocation).
46	
47	**Out**: logic changes to the conjure build pipeline (rbgjb) and the about/syft pipeline (rbgja); the vouch verify steps (download-verifier, verify-provenance) — only the vouch-push step is in; any new Lode kind; any behavior change beyond the declared validator; tool swaps (skopeo to crane); custom-builder-image migration; merging the capture spine with the made-side conjure stitch (horizon).
48	
49	**Cinched — inscribe is NOT migrated.** Inscribe's machinery stays a fork; it is relocated into its own file (relocation, not unification) and left for later cutover work to remove whole. Migrating its logic onto the spine would be throwaway and off-axis (see Premises). It joins enshrine as a doomed reference that informs the spine's shape.
50	
51	**Cinched — enshrine is reference, not migrated.** Left for later cutover work to delete; its cloud step's copy stays in place rather than migrate doomed code. The capture-cousin dedup is therefore deliberately partial until that removal.
52	
53	**Cinched — the Foundry explosion is relocation only.** Decomposing rbfl/rbfc reaches made-side machinery (step-assembly, plumb); this is acceptable because zero logic edits ride along, so behavior is trivially preserved and skirmish is a sufficient oracle. The discipline is absolute — a move carries no logic change. Annealing is a separate named pace.
54	
55	## Relocation discipline (cinched)
56	
57	Discovered in pre-implementation review; binds both host-module explosion paces — the decomposition is pure relocation only if the scaffolding is treated as copy-verbatim atoms, not as code to redistribute.
58	
59	- **Kindle/sentinel/guard is one indivisible atom per module.** Each module keeps a single entry file (`rbldk_` for rbld, `rbfck_` for rbfc) that holds the one inclusion-guard and the whole kindle and sources the guard-free body clusters; the readonly consts the kindle sets stay there and are read globally. Do not mint a guard per cluster file and do not split the kindle — both are logic edits, and per-cluster kindles break the single `ZRBFC_KINDLED` idempotency gate. (This is what "a sourced-guard always" means in practice: one guard per source-unit, on the entry file — not one per cluster.)
60	- **Leaked globals move verbatim.** The tool-image vars (`z_rbfc_tool_*`) are global-by-bare-assignment and read cross-module (rbfd reads them); adding `local`/`readonly`/`declare` while relocating silently breaks step assembly. Copy them untouched.
61	- **Flat placement only.** All cluster files stay flat siblings in `Tools/rbk/`; `BASH_SOURCE`-relative path derivations break if a cluster is nested.
62	- **Terminal exclusivity is file-level; dispatch functions keep the container prefix.** The decomposition retires the old `prefix_*.sh` files, but command and helper functions keep the container prefix — `rbld_divine`, `zrbfc_native_path_capture` move into cluster files *unchanged* (precedent: `rbho_crash_course` lives in `rbhocc_*.sh`; `buc_execute` dispatches on the single container prefix). So function CALLS are not rewritten — the forced sweep is only source-line repoints where a deleted file was named: the zipper enrollment, the Rust test driver's `source` of the old module (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`), and sibling context docs (acronym map, tabtarget / theurge context). This collapses the foundry explosion's sweep from a cross-language rename to a handful of `source`-line edits.
63	- **The multifacet CLI homes on the `0`-top, not the kindle.** When a container gains children its bare `prefix_cli.sh` retires onto `prefix0_cli.sh` (keeping `zprefix_furnish` and `buc_execute prefix_`) — the `0`-top, per the rbh0 precedent (`rbho0_cli.sh`, separate from `rbhob_base.sh`): `rbld0_`, `rbfc0_`, `rbfl0_`. The kindle-entry (`rbldk_`/`rbfck_`/`rbflk_`) is not the CLI home.
64	
65	## Verification
66	
67	Provenance-insensitivity is spiked early — read the verifier (rbgjv02) to confirm it keys on build inputs/outputs not step bytes, with one targeted cook only if ambiguous — so the spine and library invest on confirmed ground rather than discovering a broken rail at the gate. Cheap inner loop thereafter: shellcheck plus targeted fixtures, plus the validator's own unit tests; the explosion's inner loop is shellcheck plus a fixture subset, since relocation-only makes behavior trivially preserved. Milestone gate: full skirmish plus the service lode-lifecycle fixture, green with artifacts and provenance verdicts identical to pre-refactor (provenance-insensitivity-to-restructuring confirmed end-to-end). Operational precondition for the gate: live GCP credentials and a standing canonical depot must be provisioned — the gate is not free-standing. The ~100-minute skirmish cook is a milestone, not a per-commit cost; the heat pays one full cook, at the gate.
68	
69	## Minting — decided
70	
71	The open naming items are settled here: spine and bodies in the `rbldX_` family (legend in Shape); host modules decompose `rbXY_ → rbXYZ_`, the old name becoming a container and stopping naming a thing (terminal exclusivity preserved); the composition contract lives in an RBSCJ section, not a new spec letter and not RBSL (the Lode-domain spec). Precise sub-letters are not load-bearing — uniqueness and consistency are; the full allocation is recorded at the mint pace so the explosion paces inherit stable, documented targets — the explosion paces may refine cluster boundaries (which function lands in which file) but not the allocated letters. The shared cloud-step home (for vouch-push and the composed snippets) breaks the rbgj family-letter scheme since a shared step belongs to no single family — minted at the library pace.
72	
73	## Capstone — harvest CBG (Cloud Build Guide)
74	
75	A required output of this heat, produced at landing by distilling the patterns this refactor establishes — not a speculative pre-write. CBG fills the same slot for the Cloud-Build-container stack that WSG fills for the ssh-to-Windows stack: a foreign-environment sibling to BCG, sharing its philosophy (crash-fast, no silent failures, load-bearing complexity, interface contamination) but diverging entirely on mechanics. CBG operates at single-script precision — it governs the inside of one cloud step, NOT component wiring or architecture; those lessons live in the spec and this paddock.
76	
77	Contents are the cloud dialect's per-script discipline: crash-fast in the cloud idiom, the metadata-token snippet, JSON-construction rules (when hand-rolled printf is acceptable vs when jq is required), substitution naming and double-dollar escaping, idempotency under Cloud Build retry, the pure-logic/network-op split that makes a step unit-testable, and the /workspace constraints — foremost what must NOT go in it (secrets; the invariant recorded in RBSCB). CBG points at the executable contract and validator rather than restating them.
78	
79	The guide is only as good as the code it distills, so the spine, library, and per-kind bodies are written as its exemplars — quality now is what makes CBG worth harvesting. Name CBG chosen; confirm against the BCG/RCG/WSG sibling set at mint, and read WSG before authoring so the sibling framing rests on the document, not its label.
80	
81	## Done
82	
83	Bole ensconce rides one host spine and one shared cloud step-library with explicit per-step contracts; inscribe and enshrine are left as reference (not migrated); the host modules are decomposed into small, well-prefixed files (relocation only, no logic edits); the canonical token and the skopeo copy/digest block are composed snippets authored once; vouch-push is unified across Lode and hallmark-verify; the dispatch validator is in place with its own tests; full skirmish plus service pass with artifacts and provenance verdicts identical to pre-refactor (provenance-insensitivity confirmed); no new Lode kinds exist; the rationales are recorded in the Cloud Build spec — posture in RBSCB, the composition/contract decision in an RBSCJ section; and CBG (the Cloud Build Guide) is harvested from the patterns this heat establishes — see Capstone.
84	
85	## References
86	
87	- RBSCB CloudBuildPosture — skopeo-token/credential-helper and capture-duplication posture; the /workspace-no-secrets invariant.
88	- RBSCJ CloudBuildJson — JSON-composition trade study; home for the spine/contract decision (currently trigger-era stale — refresh when touched).
89	- WSG WindowsScriptingGuide — the foreign-environment sibling guide to BCG (ssh-to-Windows stack); structural precedent for CBG, to be read before CBG is authored.
90	- The bole pilot, the reliquary/inscribe and enshrine predecessors, and the cloud step family.
91	
92	# jjezs_pace ₢BXAAG
93	
94	## Character
95	Prose — recording cinched rationale alongside the code.
96	
97	## Goal
98	Record the heat's rationale in the Cloud Build specs: posture in RBSCB, the composition/contract decision as an RBSCJ section.
99	
100	## Cinched (paddock)
101	- RBSCB holds the token / credential-helper posture, the capture-duplication posture, and the /workspace-no-secrets invariant.
102	- RBSCJ is trigger-era stale; refresh it where touched.
103	
104	## Done
105	- RBSCB and RBSCJ state the cinched rationales; the step contract is documented in the RBSCJ section.

**Why we synthesize cloud reuse rather than adopt a vendor mechanism.** Cloud Build offers no native step reuse (no anchors, no includes). Its blessed mechanism — custom builder images — conflicts with the established posture that the cloud is just labor and the Director controls what enters the build: a custom builder holding our logic would be a new vouched supply-chain artifact, the opposite of inline-from-repo. So composition is a deliberate membrane, aligned with the existing stitch vocabulary rather than a competing term. Recorded in RBSCB.

**Why the metadata token stays, in-memory.** Manual metadata-server token fetch is the canonical skopeo-to-GAR authentication; docker steps auto-auth, but skopeo cannot use the gcloud helper, and docker-credential-gcr would graft a docker-ecosystem dependency into the skopeo container for the same token, with documented friction. The token is correct and irreducible; only the copy-paste is wrong — it becomes a composed snippet authored once. Invariant: /workspace carries non-secret data only — secrets are step-local and re-minted. Recorded in RBSCB.

**Why the contract plus validator are the load-bearing artifact.** Reuse runs over two channels: composed code snippets (in-memory — e.g. the canonical token and the skopeo copy/digest helper, authored once) and /workspace shared registers (non-secret data, with explicit per-step requires/provides). The disease being cured is undocumented-pattern-by-example; the cure is a documented contract plus the validator, so the codebase teaches "compose from the library," not "copy the script." The contract is recorded as a section of RBSCJ (CloudBuildJson) — cross-cutting cloud-build-composition infra, distinct from the Lode-domain spec (RBSL). Documentation is part of done, not polish.

## Shape

- **Host capture-assembly spine** (`rblds_`) — composes a build from a recipe (ordered step ids) plus a substitutions blob, submits, polls, and optionally extracts buildStepOutputs into per-capture capture-files. Takes the recipe as data; owns no kind knowledge. Lode-family, since post-inscribe every caller is a Lode kind. Built on the relocated Foundry-core build primitives (`rbfcb_`: write-script-body, wait-build-completion, native-path, git-metadata), which it calls rather than absorbs.
- **Shared cloud step-library** — the genuinely-identical whole step is **vouch-push** (rbgjl02 + rbgjv03, two live callers, the one made-side touch), unified as one library step. Shared in-step logic — the metadata-token fetch and the skopeo copy/digest block — becomes **composed snippets** the host stitches into a kind's capture step; envelope authoring (the members[] tail) stays per-kind, because each kind authors a different envelope. Kind-specific steps (inscribe's docker-mirror) stay specific and unmigrated.
- **Dispatch-time recipe validator** — the declared behavior-adding sliver; cheap guard against the expensive build; carries its own tests.
- **Shared lifecycle REST** (`rbldl_`) — divine (enumerate) and banish (delete) are direct GAR-REST host ops, NOT build-composition; kept as a distinct file from the assembly spine.
- **Per-kind body** — bole ensconce (`rbldb_`) as a thin recipe + substitutions + envelope intent riding the spine. Future kinds add sibling body files on the reserved kind-letters.
- **Host-module decomposition** (relocation only — see Scope):
  - `rbld → rbldX_` (Lode capture): `rbld0_` 0-top CLI, `rblds_` spine, `rbldb_` bole body, `rbldl_` lifecycle REST, `rbldk_` kindle-entry. Reserved for future kinds (legend only, no files this heat): `rbldt_` tool, `rbldr_` reliquary/conclave, `rbldw_` wsl, `rbldv_` podvm — kind-letters matching the Lode GAR kind-letters.
  - `rbfl → rbflX_` (Foundry Ledger): `rbfl0_` 0-top CLI plus gesture clusters (inscribe / yoke / delete / inventory / wrest) and `rbflk_` kindle-entry; letters allocated at the mint pace, cluster boundaries refined at the explosion pace.
  - `rbfc → rbfcX_` (Foundry Core, the 1576-line monolith): `rbfc0_` 0-top CLI, `rbfcv_` vessel-resolution, `rbfcb_` build-host primitives, `rbfca_` step-assembly, `rbfcg_` GAR-REST, `rbfcp_` plumb (~640 lines), `rbfck_` kindle-entry. The plumb extraction is the single biggest file win.

## Scope

**In**: the capture + vouch machinery — host spine (from bole), the cloud capture/vouch steps for ensconce, and the vouch-push unification (which legitimately touches one made-side step, hallmark-verify's rbgjv03); plus the host-module decomposition (rbld fully; rbfl and rbfc as relocation).

**Out**: logic changes to the conjure build pipeline (rbgjb) and the about/syft pipeline (rbgja); the vouch verify steps (download-verifier, verify-provenance) — only the vouch-push step is in; any new Lode kind; any behavior change beyond the declared validator; tool swaps (skopeo to crane); custom-builder-image migration; merging the capture spine with the made-side conjure stitch (horizon).

**Cinched — inscribe is NOT migrated.** Inscribe's machinery stays a fork; it is relocated into its own file (relocation, not unification) and left for later cutover work to remove whole. Migrating its logic onto the spine would be throwaway and off-axis (see Premises). It joins enshrine as a doomed reference that informs the spine's shape.

**Cinched — enshrine is reference, not migrated.** Left for later cutover work to delete; its cloud step's copy stays in place rather than migrate doomed code. The capture-cousin dedup is therefore deliberately partial until that removal.

**Cinched — the Foundry explosion is relocation only.** Decomposing rbfl/rbfc reaches made-side machinery (step-assembly, plumb); this is acceptable because zero logic edits ride along, so behavior is trivially preserved and skirmish is a sufficient oracle. The discipline is absolute — a move carries no logic change. Annealing is a separate named pace.

## Relocation discipline (cinched)

Discovered in pre-implementation review; binds both host-module explosion paces — the decomposition is pure relocation only if the scaffolding is treated as copy-verbatim atoms, not as code to redistribute.

- **Kindle/sentinel/guard is one indivisible atom per module.** Each module keeps a single entry file (`rbldk_` for rbld, `rbfck_` for rbfc) that holds the one inclusion-guard and the whole kindle and sources the guard-free body clusters; the readonly consts the kindle sets stay there and are read globally. Do not mint a guard per cluster file and do not split the kindle — both are logic edits, and per-cluster kindles break the single `ZRBFC_KINDLED` idempotency gate. (This is what "a sourced-guard always" means in practice: one guard per source-unit, on the entry file — not one per cluster.)
- **Leaked globals move verbatim.** The tool-image vars (`z_rbfc_tool_*`) are global-by-bare-assignment and read cross-module (rbfd reads them); adding `local`/`readonly`/`declare` while relocating silently breaks step assembly. Copy them untouched.
- **Flat placement only.** All cluster files stay flat siblings in `Tools/rbk/`; `BASH_SOURCE`-relative path derivations break if a cluster is nested.
- **Terminal exclusivity is file-level; dispatch functions keep the container prefix.** The decomposition retires the old `prefix_*.sh` files, but command and helper functions keep the container prefix — `rbld_divine`, `zrbfc_native_path_capture` move into cluster files *unchanged* (precedent: `rbho_crash_course` lives in `rbhocc_*.sh`; `buc_execute` dispatches on the single container prefix). So function CALLS are not rewritten — the forced sweep is only source-line repoints where a deleted file was named: the zipper enrollment, the Rust test driver's `source` of the old module (`rbtdrf_fast.rs`), and sibling context docs (acronym map, tabtarget / theurge context). This collapses the foundry explosion's sweep from a cross-language rename to a handful of `source`-line edits.
- **The multifacet CLI homes on the `0`-top, not the kindle.** When a container gains children its bare `prefix_cli.sh` retires onto `prefix0_cli.sh` (keeping `zprefix_furnish` and `buc_execute prefix_`) — the `0`-top, per the rbh0 precedent (`rbho0_cli.sh`, separate from `rbhob_base.sh`): `rbld0_`, `rbfc0_`, `rbfl0_`. The kindle-entry (`rbldk_`/`rbfck_`/`rbflk_`) is not the CLI home.

## Verification

Provenance-insensitivity is spiked early — read the verifier (rbgjv02) to confirm it keys on build inputs/outputs not step bytes, with one targeted cook only if ambiguous — so the spine and library invest on confirmed ground rather than discovering a broken rail at the gate. Cheap inner loop thereafter: shellcheck plus targeted fixtures, plus the validator's own unit tests; the explosion's inner loop is shellcheck plus a fixture subset, since relocation-only makes behavior trivially preserved. Milestone gate: full skirmish plus the service lode-lifecycle fixture, green with artifacts and provenance verdicts identical to pre-refactor (provenance-insensitivity-to-restructuring confirmed end-to-end). Operational precondition for the gate: live GCP credentials and a standing canonical depot must be provisioned — the gate is not free-standing. The ~100-minute skirmish cook is a milestone, not a per-commit cost; the heat pays one full cook, at the gate.

## Minting — decided

The open naming items are settled here: spine and bodies in the `rbldX_` family (legend in Shape); host modules decompose `rbXY_ → rbXYZ_`, the old name becoming a container and stopping naming a thing (terminal exclusivity preserved); the composition contract lives in an RBSCJ section, not a new spec letter and not RBSL (the Lode-domain spec). Precise sub-letters are not load-bearing — uniqueness and consistency are; the full allocation is recorded at the mint pace so the explosion paces inherit stable, documented targets — the explosion paces may refine cluster boundaries (which function lands in which file) but not the allocated letters. The shared cloud-step home (for vouch-push and the composed snippets) breaks the rbgj family-letter scheme since a shared step belongs to no single family — minted at the library pace.

## Capstone — harvest CBG (Cloud Build Guide)

A required output of this heat, produced at landing by distilling the patterns this refactor establishes — not a speculative pre-write. CBG fills the same slot for the Cloud-Build-container stack that WSG fills for the ssh-to-Windows stack: a foreign-environment sibling to BCG, sharing its philosophy (crash-fast, no silent failures, load-bearing complexity, interface contamination) but diverging entirely on mechanics. CBG operates at single-script precision — it governs the inside of one cloud step, NOT component wiring or architecture; those lessons live in the spec and this paddock.

Contents are the cloud dialect's per-script discipline: crash-fast in the cloud idiom, the metadata-token snippet, JSON-construction rules (when hand-rolled printf is acceptable vs when jq is required), substitution naming and double-dollar escaping, idempotency under Cloud Build retry, the pure-logic/network-op split that makes a step unit-testable, and the /workspace constraints — foremost what must NOT go in it (secrets; the invariant recorded in RBSCB). CBG points at the executable contract and validator rather than restating them.

The guide is only as good as the code it distills, so the spine, library, and per-kind bodies are written as its exemplars — quality now is what makes CBG worth harvesting. Name CBG chosen; confirm against the BCG/RCG/WSG sibling set at mint, and read WSG before authoring so the sibling framing rests on the document, not its label.

## Done

Bole ensconce rides one host spine and one shared cloud step-library with explicit per-step contracts; inscribe and enshrine are left as reference (not migrated); the host modules are decomposed into small, well-prefixed files (relocation only, no logic edits); the canonical token and the skopeo copy/digest block are composed snippets authored once; vouch-push is unified across Lode and hallmark-verify; the dispatch validator is in place with its own tests; full skirmish plus service pass with artifacts and provenance verdicts identical to pre-refactor (provenance-insensitivity confirmed); no new Lode kinds exist; the rationales are recorded in the Cloud Build spec — posture in RBSCB, the composition/contract decision in an RBSCJ section; and CBG (the Cloud Build Guide) is harvested from the patterns this heat establishes — see Capstone.

## References

- RBSCB CloudBuildPosture — skopeo-token/credential-helper and capture-duplication posture; the /workspace-no-secrets invariant.
- RBSCJ CloudBuildJson — JSON-composition trade study; home for the spine/contract decision (currently trigger-era stale — refresh when touched).
- WSG WindowsScriptingGuide — the foreign-environment sibling guide to BCG (ssh-to-Windows stack); structural precedent for CBG, to be read before CBG is authored.
- The bole pilot, the reliquary/inscribe and enshrine predecessors, and the cloud step family.

# jjezs_pace ₢BXAAG

## Character
Prose — recording cinched rationale alongside the code.

## Goal
Record the heat's rationale in the Cloud Build specs: posture in RBSCB, the composition/contract decision as an RBSCJ section.

## Cinched (paddock)
- RBSCB holds the token / credential-helper posture, the capture-duplication posture, and the /workspace-no-secrets invariant.
- RBSCJ is trigger-era stale; refresh it where touched.

## Done
- RBSCB and RBSCJ state the cinched rationales; the step contract is documented in the RBSCJ section.

1	==== Multi-Architecture Strategy Caveat
2	
3	All sections below assume Google Cloud Build (GCB) remains the sole builder.
4	GCB provides only x86 workers — default and private pools alike.
5	Multi-platform builds use QEMU emulation via the binfmt setup step (rbgjb02).
6	The binfmt step is conditionally included: vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches the build runner platform (`RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`) skip it, reducing attack surface for native-only builds.
7	
8	If a vessel requires native ARM builds, the builder strategy must expand beyond GCB, and further GCB-specific hardening should be re-evaluated.
9	
10	If native ARM builds become necessary, builder-agnostic controls (cosign signing, portable SLSA provenance) become more valuable than GCB-specific infrastructure (private pools, VPC Service Controls).
11	See cosign deferral rationale in Deferred Items.
12	
13	==== Current Posture
14	
15	Properties of the build pipeline as of 2026-03.
16	Authoritative procedures live in the operational specs (RBSAC, RBSAV, RBSDC, RBSDN);
17	this section states what holds and why it matters.
18	
19	**Build definitions are fully static.**
20	Each vessel's `cloudbuild.json` is stitched from step scripts (`rbgjb*.sh`) and current regime pins -- zero custom substitution overrides at submission time.
21	The build is fully determined by the stitched JSON and the pouch (build context as OCI image in GAR).
22	See RBSAC.
23	
24	**SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance — verified.**
25	All `builds.create` submissions produce SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance via `requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` and the `images:` field declaring per-platform image URIs.
26	Google Container Analysis generates both v0.1 and v1 predicate attestations per image.
27	Provenance chain: stitched JSON + pouch -> per-platform image digest.
28	Verification via DSSE envelope signature verification (jq + openssl against embedded GCB attestor keys).
29	
30	Production-verified build IDs:
31	
32	* Single-arch: `16d3b60f` (busybox-amd64), `fc36b970` (busybox-arm64), `9180c42a` (trbim-macos)
33	* Multi-platform: `2e172ce0` (rbev-busybox, 3 platforms — amd64, arm64, armv7)
34	
35	**Security boundary: pouch and stitched JSON.**
36	Cloud Build receives build context via the pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR) -- per-vessel Dockerfile and context files.
37	The main repo (tooling, specs, regime files) is never exposed to Google.
38	The Director controls what enters the pouch; the cloud is just labor.
39	See RBSAC.
40	
41	**Builder step images are reliquary-sourced.**
42	All GCB step images are pulled from a datestamped reliquary in GAR — co-versioned tool images frozen by the inscribe ceremony.
43	`RBRV_RELIQUARY` in each vessel regime identifies the active reliquary.
44	Base images are separately enshrined via `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`.
45	
46	**Dual private pool workers (tether + airgap).**
47	Every depot gets two private pools: a tether pool (public egress) and an airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`).
48	Pool routing is operation-level: inscribe and enshrine use tether (upstream access required), about and vouch always use airgap (verification isolated from public registries), conjure and bind use the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
49	Pools are fully managed by Google (scale to zero, no VMs to maintain).
50	
51	**Artifact Registry storage.**
52	Built images are stored in Google Artifact Registry (GAR), not Docker Hub or other external registries.
53	Regime variables configure project, region, and repository.
54	
55	**Dedicated Mason service account.**
56	Scoped to depot-project permissions, specified in the `serviceAccount` field of each build JSON.
57	
58	**SBOM generation.**
59	Per-platform Syft scans via `registry:` transport (reads directly from GAR using an OAuth2 token obtained from the GCB metadata server — no Docker daemon coupling).
60	Each platform image gets its own JSON and table-format SBOM, packaged into the per-platform metadata container.
61	Aligned with CISA 2025 minimum elements and EU CRA requirements.
62	The upgrade path is runtime-aware analysis, not removal.
63	
64	**Cloud Build logging.**
65	Build requests set `options.logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`.
66	Log hygiene (no credential echo) was audited during trigger migration script inlining.
67	
68	**Registry authentication is per-step, with no credential-helper dependency.**
69	Docker-based steps authenticate to GAR automatically — Cloud Build pre-populates Docker credentials, so no `docker login` step is needed.
70	skopeo-based steps cannot use that path (skopeo cannot consume the gcloud credential helper — gcloud is not a single binary to place on the skopeo container's PATH), so each skopeo step fetches a short-lived OAuth2 token directly from the GCB metadata server (the Mason SA's ambient identity) and passes it via `--src-creds`/`--dest-creds`.
71	This is the canonical skopeo→Artifact Registry pattern, not a workaround — the credential-helper alternative was evaluated and rejected (see Deferred Items).
72	The token is held in step-local memory and re-minted by each step that needs it; it is never written to `/workspace`.
73	_Invariant: `/workspace` carries non-secret build data only — secrets stay step-local._
74	The per-step token fetch is consequently duplicated across skopeo steps by deliberate necessity today; its planned de-duplication (one authored snippet composed into each step, never a shared on-disk secret) is tracked under Deferred Items.
75	
76	**Provenance on demand.**
77	`rbfl_tally()` lists all hallmarks and queries Container Analysis inline for each per-platform image, displaying SLSA build level, build invocation ID, and single-origin verification per hallmark.
78	Tabtarget:
79	{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}.
80	
81	===== Pipeline Architecture: Single-Platform Vessels
82	
83	Five build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step excluded at stitch time).
84	Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.
85	
86	1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components from CB substitutions; log build strategy
87	2. **build-and-load** — `docker buildx build --load` (single platform into local daemon)
88	3. **sbom** — Syft scan via `registry:` transport
89	4. **assemble-metadata** — per-platform `build_info.json` with SLSA summary fields
90	5. **build-and-push-metadata** — multi-platform `about` container pushed to `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` via `buildx --push`
91	
92	`images:` field declares the single per-platform URI → CB pushes and generates SLSA Level 3.
93	
94	===== Pipeline Architecture: Multi-Platform Vessels
95	
96	Seven build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step included at stitch time).
97	Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` differs from `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.
98	About steps (`rbgja/`) are embedded in the build job via stitch (combined image+about).
99	Vouch (`rbgjv/`) runs as a separate Cloud Build job.
100	
101	1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components; log build strategy
102	2. **qemu-binfmt** — register QEMU user-static for cross-arch emulation
103	3. **buildx-push-multi** — `docker buildx build --push` all platforms to intermediate `-multi` tag; capture `cache_before.json` (Docker daemon state pre-build) and `buildkit_metadata.json` (via `--metadata-file`)
104	4. **per-platform-pullback** — `docker pull --platform` each arch, tag individually
105	5. **push-per-platform** — `docker push` each per-platform tag (pre-push for imagetools); capture `cache_after.json` (Docker daemon state post-push)
106	6. **imagetools-create** — reassemble consumer-facing manifest list from per-platform tags
107	7. **push-diags** — build `FROM scratch` container with build-time diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`), push as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`
108	
109	`images:` field declares all per-platform URIs → CB re-pushes (idempotent) and generates SLSA Level 3.
110	The reassembled manifest list provides transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform images.
111	
112	===== Tag Scheme
113	
114	[cols="1,2",options="header"]
115	|===
116	|Tag Pattern
117	|Purpose
118	
119	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{INSCRIBE_TS}-multi`
120	|Intermediate `buildx --push` target (multi-platform only)
121	
122	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{HALLMARK}-{SUFFIX}`
123	|Per-platform image tag on the image package (persistent, hallmark-tagged)
124	
125	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{INSCRIBE_TS}-{SUFFIX}`
126	|SLSA alias tag (`images:` field, provenance generation trigger — ephemeral, overwritten on re-conjure)
127	
128	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{HALLMARK}`
129	|Consumer-facing manifest list on the image package (reassembled by `imagetools create`, multi-platform only)
130	
131	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/about:{HALLMARK}`
132	|Metadata container on the about package (build provenance — multi-platform, see About Artifact Contents below)
133	
134	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/diags:{HALLMARK}`
135	|Build-time diagnostics container on the diags package (conjure only — `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`). Pushed by conjure step 07, pulled by about step 01.
136	
137	|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/attest:{HALLMARK}-{SUFFIX}`
138	|Per-platform attestation tags on the single attest package (one tag per architecture).
139	|===
140	
141	Platform suffix derivation: `linux/amd64` → `amd64`, `linux/arm64` → `arm64`, `linux/arm/v7` → `armv7`.
142	Computed at inscribe time from `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS`.
143	
144	===== About Artifact Contents
145	
146	The `about` artifact (at `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>`) is a `FROM scratch` multi-platform container carrying build provenance files at the root filesystem.
147	Contents are extracted by
148	{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
149	for trust posture display.
150	
151	[cols="1,2",options="header"]
152	|===
153	|File
154	|Contents
155	
156	|`build_info.json`
157	|Per-platform build metadata: hallmark, image URI, moniker, platform, QEMU usage, git coordinates (repo/branch/commit), build timestamps, and SLSA summary (build level, invocation ID, builder ID, predicate types).
158	
159	|`sbom.json`
160	|Syft SPDX JSON output: package inventory with type, name, version, license, and Package URL for every detected package.
161	
162	|`recipe.txt`
163	|The exact Dockerfile used to build the image.
164	
165	|`buildkit_metadata.json`
166	|BuildKit resolved base image references and build parameters (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).
167	
168	|`cache_before.json`
169	|Docker daemon image state before the build (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).
170	
171	|`cache_after.json`
172	|Docker daemon image state after all pushes (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).
173	
174	|===
175	
176	NOTE: The three enrichment files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`) are present only for conjure hallmarks.
177	They are forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` registry package by the about pipeline's step 1.
178	For bind and graft, these files are absent and the about artifact contains only `build_info.json`, `sbom.json`, and optionally `recipe.txt`.
179	
180	===== OCI Layout Bridge — Superseded
181	
182	The original OCI Layout Bridge (RBSOB) — export to OCI tarball, push via crane, split with skopeo — is fully superseded for all vessel types.
183	Single-platform vessels use `buildx --load` into the local daemon.
184	Multi-platform vessels use `buildx --push` with per-platform pullback.
185	CB pre-populates GAR credentials; no explicit docker login step is needed.
186	See RBSOB superseded notice.
187	
188	==== Implemented: Egress Lockdown via Dual Pools
189	
190	Egress lockdown is implemented through the dual-pool architecture.
191	The airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`) blocks all public internet access.
192	Operations that require upstream access (inscribe, enshrine) run on the tether pool.
193	Operations that must be isolated (about, vouch) always run on the airgap pool.
194	Conjure and bind are routed per vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
195	
196	**Dependency mirroring is universal.**
197	All GCB step images come from reliquaries (inscribed tool images in GAR).
198	All base images come from anchors (enshrined base images in GAR).
199	This is not just an airgap feature — it is a universal robustness measure against upstream registry churn.
200	
201	**Operation pool routing:**
202	
203	[cols="1,1,2",options="header"]
204	|===
205	|Operation
206	|Pool
207	|Rationale
208	
209	|Inscribe
210	|tether
211	|Pulls tool images from upstream registries
212	
213	|Enshrine
214	|tether
215	|Pulls base images from upstream via skopeo
216	
217	|Conjure/Bind
218	|vessel's RBRV_EGRESS_MODE
219	|Build-time deps vary per vessel
220	
221	|About
222	|airgap (always)
223	|Only needs GAR + Google APIs (Private Google Access)
224	
225	|Vouch
226	|airgap (always)
227	|Verification isolated from public registries
228	|===
229	
230	**About/vouch always-airgap is a security property**: verification can never be influenced by a compromised public registry, even for tether vessels.
231	
232	==== Potential Hardening: VPC Service Controls Perimeter
233	
234	Wrap the depot project in a VPC Service Controls (VPC-SC) perimeter.
235	This restricts which GCP APIs can be called and from where, reducing data exfiltration risk.
236	
237	**Required infrastructure:**
238	
239	* VPC-SC perimeter around the depot project (organizational policy — may require org-level admin)
240	* Access levels for operator workstations (so builds can still be submitted from outside the perimeter)
241	* Ingress/egress rules for the perimeter (which services may cross the boundary)
242	* Testing: every GCP API call in the pipeline must be verified inside the perimeter — Cloud Logging writes, GCS uploads, Artifact Registry pushes, Cloud Build API calls can break subtly
243	
244	**Prerequisite:** Egress Lockdown is in place (dual-pool architecture).
245	
246	==== Deferred Items
247	
248	Evaluated and consciously deferred; each has a specific revisit trigger.
249	
250	**Dockerfile base image digest validation.**
251	Builder step images are digest-pinned (Current Posture), but user-authored Dockerfiles in vessel build contexts are not validated.
252	A vessel with `FROM ubuntu:22.04` passes today — mutable tag, supply-chain risk.
253	This constrains vessel authors (policy decision with RBSRV implications), orthogonal to pipeline infrastructure.
254	Separate future heat.
255	
256	**BuildKit provenance flag.**
257	`--provenance=true` on `docker buildx build` records builder internals — useful for deeper forensics.
258	GCB-native SLSA v1.0 provenance covers the primary attestation use case.
259	Redundant for the current threat model.
260	Revisit if build reproducibility verification becomes a priority.
261	
262	**Cosign image signing.**
263	Cryptographic signatures attached to images, verifiable by downstream consumers.
264	Two modes: keyless (Sigstore/Fulcio — requires public internet, conflicts with Egress Lockdown) and keyed (Cloud KMS — no external dependency).
265	With CB-native SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 verified on all vessel types, cosign is redundant for the current threat model (building and consuming within own GCP project).
266	Becomes valuable if: images are distributed externally, trust verification needed outside GCP, or builder strategy expands beyond GCB (losing Google's provenance).
267	If adopted, prefer KMS-backed keyed signing (`cosign sign --key gcpkms://...`) for Egress Lockdown compatibility.
268	
269	**Binary Authorization.**
270	Deploy-time policy enforcement: only attested images can be deployed.
271	Relevant for GKE and Cloud Run.
272	Now technically achievable — CB-native SLSA Level 3 satisfies BinAuth attestation requirements.
273	Recipe Bottle deploys to Podman on workstations — no benefit until deployment targets change.
274	
275	**Reproducibility verification.**
276	Periodic independent rebuilds compared by digest.
277	Requires reproducible toolchains — a vessel-author discipline, not a pipeline control.
278	Not actionable until vessel ecosystem matures.
279	
280	**skopeo credential helper — rejected, not merely deferred (evaluated 2026-06).**
281	The standalone `docker-credential-gcr` helper could let skopeo authenticate to GAR via a `credHelpers` config entry instead of the inline token fetch (see Current Posture).
282	Rejected: on GCB the helper mints the _same_ metadata-server token we already obtain (no new capability), but requires injecting a new binary into the skopeo container — a fresh vouched supply-chain dependency, the opposite of our minimize-the-dependency-manifold posture — and skopeo→GAR via credential helper carries documented 400-error friction (skopeo issue #2573, locked/unresolved), with the community fallback being the explicit `oauth2accesstoken` token, i.e. exactly the current approach.
283	Revisit only if skopeo gains first-class GAR/ADC authentication or the helper friction is resolved upstream.
284	
285	**Capture-machinery duplication — token fetch, skopeo-capture, vouch-push.**
286	The cloud capture steps presently duplicate logic by copy: the metadata-token fetch across every skopeo step, the inspect→digest→fingerprint→copy block (enshrine and ensconce), and the `FROM scratch` vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02` and hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`).
287	This is acknowledged, not accidental — Cloud Build offers no native step reuse (no YAML anchors or includes), and its blessed reuse mechanism, custom builder images, conflicts with the _cloud is just labor; the Director controls what enters_ posture above (a custom builder holding our logic would be a new vouched artifact rather than inline-from-repo).
288	Planned remedy: a host assembly spine (aligned with the `stitch` vocabulary) plus a shared cloud step-library and a dispatch-time recipe validator that derives each step's substitution coverage — composed once and referenced, replacing copy-by-example with compose-from-library. The `/workspace` inter-step channel stays guarded in-step (the steps' own `test -f` preconditions) rather than by a dispatch contract.
289	Scoped to a dedicated behavior-preserving refactor heat (full skirmish + service as the oracle); resolve there.
290

==== Multi-Architecture Strategy Caveat

All sections below assume Google Cloud Build (GCB) remains the sole builder.
GCB provides only x86 workers — default and private pools alike.
Multi-platform builds use QEMU emulation via the binfmt setup step (rbgjb02).
The binfmt step is conditionally included: vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches the build runner platform (`RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`) skip it, reducing attack surface for native-only builds.

If a vessel requires native ARM builds, the builder strategy must expand beyond GCB, and further GCB-specific hardening should be re-evaluated.

If native ARM builds become necessary, builder-agnostic controls (cosign signing, portable SLSA provenance) become more valuable than GCB-specific infrastructure (private pools, VPC Service Controls).
See cosign deferral rationale in Deferred Items.

==== Current Posture

Properties of the build pipeline as of 2026-03.
Authoritative procedures live in the operational specs (RBSAC, RBSAV, RBSDC, RBSDN);
this section states what holds and why it matters.

**Build definitions are fully static.**
Each vessel's `cloudbuild.json` is stitched from step scripts (`rbgjb*.sh`) and current regime pins -- zero custom substitution overrides at submission time.
The build is fully determined by the stitched JSON and the pouch (build context as OCI image in GAR).
See RBSAC.

**SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance — verified.**
All `builds.create` submissions produce SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 provenance via `requestedVerifyOption: VERIFIED` and the `images:` field declaring per-platform image URIs.
Google Container Analysis generates both v0.1 and v1 predicate attestations per image.
Provenance chain: stitched JSON + pouch -> per-platform image digest.
Verification via DSSE envelope signature verification (jq + openssl against embedded GCB attestor keys).

Production-verified build IDs:

* Single-arch: `16d3b60f` (busybox-amd64), `fc36b970` (busybox-arm64), `9180c42a` (trbim-macos)
* Multi-platform: `2e172ce0` (rbev-busybox, 3 platforms — amd64, arm64, armv7)

**Security boundary: pouch and stitched JSON.**
Cloud Build receives build context via the pouch (FROM SCRATCH OCI image in GAR) -- per-vessel Dockerfile and context files.
The main repo (tooling, specs, regime files) is never exposed to Google.
The Director controls what enters the pouch; the cloud is just labor.
See RBSAC.

**Builder step images are reliquary-sourced.**
All GCB step images are pulled from a datestamped reliquary in GAR — co-versioned tool images frozen by the inscribe ceremony.
`RBRV_RELIQUARY` in each vessel regime identifies the active reliquary.
Base images are separately enshrined via `RBRV_IMAGE_n_ANCHOR`.

**Dual private pool workers (tether + airgap).**
Every depot gets two private pools: a tether pool (public egress) and an airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`).
Pool routing is operation-level: inscribe and enshrine use tether (upstream access required), about and vouch always use airgap (verification isolated from public registries), conjure and bind use the vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.
Pools are fully managed by Google (scale to zero, no VMs to maintain).

**Artifact Registry storage.**
Built images are stored in Google Artifact Registry (GAR), not Docker Hub or other external registries.
Regime variables configure project, region, and repository.

**Dedicated Mason service account.**
Scoped to depot-project permissions, specified in the `serviceAccount` field of each build JSON.

**SBOM generation.**
Per-platform Syft scans via `registry:` transport (reads directly from GAR using an OAuth2 token obtained from the GCB metadata server — no Docker daemon coupling).
Each platform image gets its own JSON and table-format SBOM, packaged into the per-platform metadata container.
Aligned with CISA 2025 minimum elements and EU CRA requirements.
The upgrade path is runtime-aware analysis, not removal.

**Cloud Build logging.**
Build requests set `options.logging: CLOUD_LOGGING_ONLY`.
Log hygiene (no credential echo) was audited during trigger migration script inlining.

**Registry authentication is per-step, with no credential-helper dependency.**
Docker-based steps authenticate to GAR automatically — Cloud Build pre-populates Docker credentials, so no `docker login` step is needed.
skopeo-based steps cannot use that path (skopeo cannot consume the gcloud credential helper — gcloud is not a single binary to place on the skopeo container's PATH), so each skopeo step fetches a short-lived OAuth2 token directly from the GCB metadata server (the Mason SA's ambient identity) and passes it via `--src-creds`/`--dest-creds`.
This is the canonical skopeo→Artifact Registry pattern, not a workaround — the credential-helper alternative was evaluated and rejected (see Deferred Items).
The token is held in step-local memory and re-minted by each step that needs it; it is never written to `/workspace`.
_Invariant: `/workspace` carries non-secret build data only — secrets stay step-local._
The per-step token fetch is consequently duplicated across skopeo steps by deliberate necessity today; its planned de-duplication (one authored snippet composed into each step, never a shared on-disk secret) is tracked under Deferred Items.

**Provenance on demand.**
`rbfl_tally()` lists all hallmarks and queries Container Analysis inline for each per-platform image, displaying SLSA build level, build invocation ID, and single-origin verification per hallmark.
Tabtarget:
{rbtgo_hallmark_tally}.

===== Pipeline Architecture: Single-Platform Vessels

Five build steps plus CB-native `images:` push (binfmt step excluded at stitch time).
Applies to vessels whose `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` matches `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM`.

1. **derive-tag-base** — compute timestamps and tag components from CB substitutions; log build strategy
2. **build-and-load** — `docker buildx build --load` (single platform into local daemon)
3. **sbom** — Syft scan via `registry:` transport
4. **assemble-metadata** — per-platform `build_info.json` with SLSA summary fields
5. **build-and-push-metadata** — multi-platform `about` container pushed to `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>` via `buildx --push`

===== Pipeline Architecture: Multi-Platform Vessels

Vouch (`rbgjv/`) runs as a separate Cloud Build job.

7. **push-diags** — build `FROM scratch` container with build-time diagnostic files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`), push as `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/«HALLMARK»/diags:«HALLMARK»`

`images:` field declares all per-platform URIs → CB re-pushes (idempotent) and generates SLSA Level 3.
The reassembled manifest list provides transparent platform resolution to attested per-platform images.

===== Tag Scheme

[cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
|Tag Pattern
|Purpose

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{INSCRIBE_TS}-multi`
|Intermediate `buildx --push` target (multi-platform only)

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{HALLMARK}-{SUFFIX}`
|Per-platform image tag on the image package (persistent, hallmark-tagged)

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{INSCRIBE_TS}-{SUFFIX}`

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/image:{HALLMARK}`
|Consumer-facing manifest list on the image package (reassembled by `imagetools create`, multi-platform only)

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/about:{HALLMARK}`
|Metadata container on the about package (build provenance — multi-platform, see About Artifact Contents below)

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/diags:{HALLMARK}`
|Build-time diagnostics container on the diags package (conjure only — `buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`, `recipe.txt`). Pushed by conjure step 07, pulled by about step 01.

|`{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/{HALLMARK}/attest:{HALLMARK}-{SUFFIX}`
|Per-platform attestation tags on the single attest package (one tag per architecture).
|===

Platform suffix derivation: `linux/amd64` → `amd64`, `linux/arm64` → `arm64`, `linux/arm/v7` → `armv7`.
Computed at inscribe time from `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS`.

===== About Artifact Contents

The `about` artifact (at `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/about:<hallmark>`) is a `FROM scratch` multi-platform container carrying build provenance files at the root filesystem.
Contents are extracted by
{rbtgo_ark_inspect}
for trust posture display.

[cols="1,2",options="header"]
|===
|File
|Contents

|`build_info.json`
|Per-platform build metadata: hallmark, image URI, moniker, platform, QEMU usage, git coordinates (repo/branch/commit), build timestamps, and SLSA summary (build level, invocation ID, builder ID, predicate types).

|`sbom.json`
|Syft SPDX JSON output: package inventory with type, name, version, license, and Package URL for every detected package.

|`recipe.txt`
|The exact Dockerfile used to build the image.

|`buildkit_metadata.json`
|BuildKit resolved base image references and build parameters (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).

|`cache_before.json`
|Docker daemon image state before the build (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).

|`cache_after.json`
|Docker daemon image state after all pushes (conjure only, forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` package).

|===

NOTE: The three enrichment files (`buildkit_metadata.json`, `cache_before.json`, `cache_after.json`) are present only for conjure hallmarks.
They are forwarded from the `{gar_hallmarks_namespace}/<hallmark>/diags` registry package by the about pipeline's step 1.
For bind and graft, these files are absent and the about artifact contains only `build_info.json`, `sbom.json`, and optionally `recipe.txt`.

===== OCI Layout Bridge — Superseded

The original OCI Layout Bridge (RBSOB) — export to OCI tarball, push via crane, split with skopeo — is fully superseded for all vessel types.
Single-platform vessels use `buildx --load` into the local daemon.
Multi-platform vessels use `buildx --push` with per-platform pullback.
CB pre-populates GAR credentials; no explicit docker login step is needed.
See RBSOB superseded notice.

==== Implemented: Egress Lockdown via Dual Pools

Egress lockdown is implemented through the dual-pool architecture.
The airgap pool (`NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS`) blocks all public internet access.
Operations that require upstream access (inscribe, enshrine) run on the tether pool.
Operations that must be isolated (about, vouch) always run on the airgap pool.
Conjure and bind are routed per vessel's `RBRV_EGRESS_MODE`.

**Dependency mirroring is universal.**
All GCB step images come from reliquaries (inscribed tool images in GAR).
All base images come from anchors (enshrined base images in GAR).
This is not just an airgap feature — it is a universal robustness measure against upstream registry churn.

**Operation pool routing:**

[cols="1,1,2",options="header"]
|===
|Operation
|Pool
|Rationale

|Inscribe
|tether
|Pulls tool images from upstream registries

|Enshrine
|tether
|Pulls base images from upstream via skopeo

|Conjure/Bind
|vessel's RBRV_EGRESS_MODE
|Build-time deps vary per vessel

|About
|airgap (always)
|Only needs GAR + Google APIs (Private Google Access)

|Vouch
|airgap (always)
|Verification isolated from public registries
|===

**About/vouch always-airgap is a security property**: verification can never be influenced by a compromised public registry, even for tether vessels.

==== Potential Hardening: VPC Service Controls Perimeter

Wrap the depot project in a VPC Service Controls (VPC-SC) perimeter.
This restricts which GCP APIs can be called and from where, reducing data exfiltration risk.

**Required infrastructure:**

* VPC-SC perimeter around the depot project (organizational policy — may require org-level admin)
* Access levels for operator workstations (so builds can still be submitted from outside the perimeter)
* Ingress/egress rules for the perimeter (which services may cross the boundary)
* Testing: every GCP API call in the pipeline must be verified inside the perimeter — Cloud Logging writes, GCS uploads, Artifact Registry pushes, Cloud Build API calls can break subtly

**Prerequisite:** Egress Lockdown is in place (dual-pool architecture).

==== Deferred Items

Evaluated and consciously deferred; each has a specific revisit trigger.

**Dockerfile base image digest validation.**
Builder step images are digest-pinned (Current Posture), but user-authored Dockerfiles in vessel build contexts are not validated.
A vessel with `FROM ubuntu:22.04` passes today — mutable tag, supply-chain risk.
This constrains vessel authors (policy decision with RBSRV implications), orthogonal to pipeline infrastructure.
Separate future heat.

**BuildKit provenance flag.**
`--provenance=true` on `docker buildx build` records builder internals — useful for deeper forensics.
GCB-native SLSA v1.0 provenance covers the primary attestation use case.
Redundant for the current threat model.
Revisit if build reproducibility verification becomes a priority.

**Cosign image signing.**
Cryptographic signatures attached to images, verifiable by downstream consumers.
Two modes: keyless (Sigstore/Fulcio — requires public internet, conflicts with Egress Lockdown) and keyed (Cloud KMS — no external dependency).
With CB-native SLSA v1.0 Build Level 3 verified on all vessel types, cosign is redundant for the current threat model (building and consuming within own GCP project).
Becomes valuable if: images are distributed externally, trust verification needed outside GCP, or builder strategy expands beyond GCB (losing Google's provenance).
If adopted, prefer KMS-backed keyed signing (`cosign sign --key gcpkms://...`) for Egress Lockdown compatibility.

**Binary Authorization.**
Deploy-time policy enforcement: only attested images can be deployed.
Relevant for GKE and Cloud Run.
Now technically achievable — CB-native SLSA Level 3 satisfies BinAuth attestation requirements.
Recipe Bottle deploys to Podman on workstations — no benefit until deployment targets change.

**Reproducibility verification.**
Periodic independent rebuilds compared by digest.
Requires reproducible toolchains — a vessel-author discipline, not a pipeline control.
Not actionable until vessel ecosystem matures.

**skopeo credential helper — rejected, not merely deferred (evaluated 2026-06).**
The standalone `docker-credential-gcr` helper could let skopeo authenticate to GAR via a `credHelpers` config entry instead of the inline token fetch (see Current Posture).
Rejected: on GCB the helper mints the _same_ metadata-server token we already obtain (no new capability), but requires injecting a new binary into the skopeo container — a fresh vouched supply-chain dependency, the opposite of our minimize-the-dependency-manifold posture — and skopeo→GAR via credential helper carries documented 400-error friction (skopeo issue #2573, locked/unresolved), with the community fallback being the explicit `oauth2accesstoken` token, i.e. exactly the current approach.
Revisit only if skopeo gains first-class GAR/ADC authentication or the helper friction is resolved upstream.

**Capture-machinery duplication — token fetch, skopeo-capture, vouch-push.**
The cloud capture steps presently duplicate logic by copy: the metadata-token fetch across every skopeo step, the inspect→digest→fingerprint→copy block (enshrine and ensconce), and the `FROM scratch` vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02` and hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`).
This is acknowledged, not accidental — Cloud Build offers no native step reuse (no YAML anchors or includes), and its blessed reuse mechanism, custom builder images, conflicts with the _cloud is just labor; the Director controls what enters_ posture above (a custom builder holding our logic would be a new vouched artifact rather than inline-from-repo).
Planned remedy: a host assembly spine (aligned with the `stitch` vocabulary) plus a shared cloud step-library and a dispatch-time recipe validator that derives each step's substitution coverage — composed once and referenced, replacing copy-by-example with compose-from-library. The `/workspace` inter-step channel stays guarded in-step (the steps' own `test -f` preconditions) rather than by a dispatch contract.
Scoped to a dedicated behavior-preserving refactor heat (full skirmish + service as the oracle); resolve there.

==== References

**Google Cloud documentation:**

* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/securing-builds/generate-validate-build-provenance[Generate and validate build provenance (SLSA)]
* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/private-pools-overview[Private Pools overview]
* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/private-pool-config-file-schema[Private Pool configuration schema]
* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/use-in-private-network[Cloud Build in a private network (NO_PUBLIC_EGRESS)]
* https://docs.google.com/build/docs/private-pools/using-vpc-service-controls[VPC Service Controls with Cloud Build]
* https://cloud.google.com/binary-authorization/docs/deploy-cloud-build[Deploy only images built by Cloud Build (Binary Authorization)]
* https://cloud.google.com/build/pricing-update[Cloud Build pricing update (2025)]

**SBOM and supply chain:**

* https://www.cisa.gov/resources-tools/resources/2025-minimum-elements-software-bill-materials-sbom[CISA 2025 Minimum Elements for SBOM]
* https://www.cisa.gov/sbom[CISA SBOM overview]
* https://openssf.org/blog/2025/06/05/choosing-an-sbom-generation-tool/[OpenSSF: Choosing an SBOM Generation Tool]

**Signing and attestation:**

* https://docs.sigstore.dev/cosign/signing/signing_with_containers/[Cosign container signing]
* https://slsa.dev/provenance/v1[SLSA Provenance v1.0 specification]

**Registry authentication (skopeo / credential helpers):**

* https://github.com/containers/skopeo/issues/2573[skopeo #2573 — Artifact Registry credential-helper 400 error (locked)]
* https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/docker-credential-gcr[docker-credential-gcr — standalone, gcloud-SDK-independent GCR/GAR credential helper]
* https://cloud.google.com/blog/topics/developers-practitioners/five-ways-skopeo-can-simplify-your-google-cloud-container-workflow/[Five ways Skopeo can simplify your Google Cloud container workflow]
* https://docs.cloud.google.com/artifact-registry/docs/docker/authentication[Configure authentication to Artifact Registry for Docker]

**Internal references:**

* RBSOB: OCI Layout Bridge trade study (superseded)
* RBSCJ: Cloud Build JSON trade study
* RBSTB: Trigger Build specification (inactive -- triggers eliminated, builds.create replaces)
* rbfd_FoundryDirectorBuild.sh: `zrbfd_stitch_build_json()` — build stitching; `rbfl_tally()` — provenance on demand; `rbfc_plumb_full()`/`rbfc_plumb_compact()` — local trust posture display
* RBSAI: Ark Inspect specification
* buv_validation.sh: `buv_val_odref()` — digest-pinned image reference validator
* RBRV vessel regime: `RBRV_CONJURE_PLATFORMS` vs `RBGC_BUILD_RUNNER_PLATFORM` — binfmt inclusion predicate
* Memos/memo-20260305-provenance-architecture-gap.md — provenance experiment evidence
