1	==== Decision
2	
3	Cloud Build configuration files for trigger-based builds use `cloudbuild.json` format,
4	generated programmatically via `jq`.
5	YAML is not used.
6	
7	==== Rationale
8	
9	JSON is the Cloud Build REST API's native wire format.
10	The `projects.builds.create` and `projects.triggers.run` endpoints accept JSON request bodies
11	modeled on the API's `Build` resource.
12	YAML is a file-authoring convenience that gets parsed into the same `Build` structure before submission.
13	
14	Programmatic generation via `jq` produces correct JSON directly — no YAML indentation
15	fragility, no multiline string quoting hazards, no dependency on `yq` or custom emitters.
16	The stitch function (`zrbf_stitch_build_json`) already generates JSON;
17	converting to YAML would add complexity with zero functional benefit.
18	
19	==== Evidence
20	
21	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
22	|===
23	|Claim
24	|Source
25	
26	|REST API is natively JSON
27	|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.builds/create[projects.builds.create API reference] — request body is a `Build` resource in JSON
28	
29	|YAML and JSON are equivalent serializations
30	|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/build-config-file-schema[Build configuration file schema] — "You can write the build config file using the YAML or the JSON syntax"
31	
32	|Triggers accept both formats
33	|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/automating-builds/create-manage-triggers[Create and manage build triggers] — "Cloud Build configuration file (yaml or json)"
34	
35	|Trigger autodetect precedence includes JSON
36	|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.triggers[projects.triggers REST resource] — precedence: `cloudbuild.yaml`, `cloudbuild.yml`, `cloudbuild.json`, `Dockerfile`
37	
38	|No deprecation signals through Feb 2026
39	|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/release-notes[Cloud Build release notes] — no mention of reducing JSON support
40	
41	|SLSA provenance does not differ by format
42	|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/securing-builds/generate-validate-build-provenance[Generate and validate build provenance] — provenance records `buildConfigSource.path` (file name), not format-specific fields
43	|===
44	
45	==== What Dies
46	
47	* Any assumption that `cloudbuild.yaml` is required for triggers or SLSA provenance
48	* The linked term `rbtgr_build_yaml` — replaced by
49	{rbtgr_build_json}
50	* YAML-specific concerns: multiline string quoting, indentation sensitivity, `$$` escaping conventions
51	
52	==== What Survives
53	
54	* `zrbf_stitch_build_json()` — continues to emit JSON via `jq`, adapted for trigger structure
55	* `rbgjb/*.sh` step scripts — inlined into JSON `args` arrays at inscribe time
56	* All `_RBGY_*` substitution variables — baked into JSON `substitutions` object
57	* OCI Layout Bridge pattern — unchanged
58	
59	==== Provenance Note
60	
61	SLSA v1.0 provenance records `buildConfigSource.path` which will contain `cloudbuild.json`.
62	This is a cosmetic difference from `cloudbuild.yaml` — the structured provenance fields
63	(build inputs, parameters, outputs) are identical regardless of config file format.
64	
65	==== References
66	
67	* Research conducted: 2026-02-24 (web search + API documentation review)
68	* Google docs consistently present YAML examples by convention, not by requirement
69	* The `gcloud builds submit` CLI internally converts any config format to JSON for API submission
70	
71	==== Capture Composition Contract (rblds_ spine)
72	
73	This section governs the host-composed `projects.builds.create` path for Lode
74	captures — distinct from the trigger-autodetect path the Decision above covers.
75	A Lode capture is composed on the host by the capture-assembly spine
76	(`rblds_Spine.sh`) from two data inputs handed by a per-kind body (e.g. bole's
77	`zrbld_ensconce_submit`): an ordered _recipe_ of step rows and an opaque
78	_substitutions_ blob. The spine carries no per-kind branch and no
79	kind-registration mechanism — a new Lode kind is a new body file the spine
80	consumes as data, never a conditional inside the spine.
81	
82	===== Spine/body boundary
83	
84	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
85	|===
86	|Owner |Carries
87	
88	|Spine (`rblds_`)
89	|The step-composition loop (recipe rows -> `steps[]`); the Build-resource
90	envelope shape (`serviceAccount`, `options.automapSubstitutions`,
91	`options.logging`, `options.pool`, `timeout`); submit + build-id persist +
92	console link; the poll via `zrbfc_wait_build_completion`; and the
93	`buildStepOutputs[N]` base64-decode. The capture-domain constants — the mason
94	capture SA, the `TETHER` pool, the regime build timeout — are spine-owned,
95	because every Lode capture fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network.
96	
97	|Body (`rbld«kind»_`)
98	|The recipe rows (pre-resolved); the substitutions blob; the poll ceiling (the
99	one kind-flavored build knob); and the interpretation of the decoded step
100	output (the per-member envelope fan-out).
101	|===
102	
103	The only kind-flavored build knob the body passes is the poll ceiling (ensconce
104	borrows the enshrine ceiling). `serviceAccount`/`pool`/`timeout` are NOT body
105	parameters: they are capture-domain invariants the spine owns. A future kind
106	that needed a different pool or SA would change the spine's capture-domain
107	constants deliberately, not slip past as recipe data.
108	
109	===== Recipe row format
110	
111	A recipe is an ordered list of `|`-delimited step rows. The `|` delimiter is
112	load-bearing: builder image refs carry colons (tags and `sha256:` digests), so a
113	colon delimiter would be ambiguous.
114	
115	----
116	script_path | builder_image | id | entrypoint
117	----
118	
119	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
120	|===
121	|Field |Meaning
122	
123	|`script_path`   |Absolute path to the step script, _pre-resolved by the body_. The spine owns no steps-directory knowledge.
124	|`builder_image` |The step's builder image ref (a reliquary tool image).
125	|`id`            |The Cloud Build step id.
126	|`entrypoint`    |`bash` \| `sh` \| `python3` — selects the composed shebang line. Any other value fails composition.
127	|===
128	
129	The spine reads each script's body (shebang stripped), expands any
130	`#@rbgjs_include` markers against the shared snippet library (see _Composed-snippet
131	library_ below), prepends the shebang the entrypoint selects, and inlines the
132	result as the step's `script` field. This is the same shape the made-side
133	`rbfca_` assemblers use; the capture spine is the canonical single copy for the
134	fetched side.
135	
136	===== Substitutions blob — opaque
137	
138	The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
139	spine slots it verbatim into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and
140	reads no key from it. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each
141	step's environment. The spine's opacity to the blob is what keeps it
142	registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
143	spine.
144	
145	Invariant (RBSCB): substitutions and `/workspace` carry non-secret data only;
146	secrets are step-local and re-minted.
147	
148	===== Extract contract
149	
150	The spine's extract decodes one step's `buildStepOutputs[N]` slot — base64 to a
151	destination file — where `N` is the only generic parameter. The decoded JSON's
152	shape (for bole: `slot_1`..`slot_N` member envelopes, each a stamp + vouch) is
153	the body's knowledge; the spine is member-count-agnostic. This keeps the
154	N-member envelope axis out of the spine.
155	
156	===== Substitution-coverage check (enforced by zrbld_spine_validate)
157	
158	A composed step reads two register channels:
159	
160	* _Substitution registers_ — `_RBGL_*` keys present in the substitutions blob,
161	  automapped into every step's environment.
162	* _/workspace registers_ — non-secret files a step writes for a later step,
163	  named by path relative to `/workspace`.
164	
165	The two channels are validated differently, because they differ in what can be
166	recovered safely from the step source. The substitution channel — where an
167	undefined reference is both silent (it expands to empty in-build) and expensive
168	to surface (it costs the whole cook) — is checked by derivation at dispatch; the
169	`/workspace` channel is left to the steps' own in-build guards.
170	
171	*Substitution channel — derived, not declared.* A step's substitution _requires_
172	is exactly the set of `_RBGL_*` names its body references — the references ARE the
173	requires, so nothing separate is declared and nothing can drift from the code. At
174	dispatch `zrbld_spine_validate` scans each step's include-expanded body for
175	`_RBGL_*` tokens on non-comment lines and fails before submit at the first token
176	absent from the substitutions blob's keys, naming the offending register. Coverage
177	is _flat_: substitutions automap into every step, so a register is satisfied by
178	its presence in the blob alone — there is no recipe order and no cross-step
179	`provides`. The scan runs on the _include-expanded_ body; composed snippets carry
180	no `_RBGL_*` references (they speak the shell-var channel — see below), so only the
181	kind's own substitution reads are recovered.
182	
183	*Workspace channel — guarded in-step, dispatch-validation deferred.* A step's
184	`/workspace` reads and writes are not safely recoverable by a static scan: the
185	paths are templated by runtime loop variables (e.g. `lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json`)
186	and read-vs-write classification in shell (redirections, `test -f`, `cp` of a
187	var-held path) is exactly what a scanner gets wrong. The live recipes already
188	guard their own `/workspace` preconditions at runtime — the vouch-push step opens
189	with `test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt || FATAL "step 01 must run first"` — so
190	the channel is crash-fast in-build. A dispatch-time `/workspace` declaration is
191	added only when a future kind needs one, not authored speculatively (generalize
192	against the instances we have, never stub the ones we don't).
193	
194	The derivation is shaped by the capture domain's three axes:
195	
196	* _N-member envelopes_ — the terminal step's single `buildStepOutputs` slot is
197	  body knowledge (member fan-out lives in the body), so it never enters the
198	  coverage check.
199	* _Tool variety_ — a `_RBGL_*` name appears as the same literal token whether a
200	  step is bash (`${_RBGL_X}`) or `python3` (`os.environ['_RBGL_X']`), so the scan
201	  is identical across builder image and entrypoint.
202	* _Opaque-blob payloads_ — the check tests key _presence_ by NAME, never value
203	  shape, preserving the spine's opacity to the blob.
204	
205	===== Composed-snippet library (rbgjs)
206	
207	Cloud Build offers no native step reuse. Where the same in-step logic recurs
208	across cloud steps, it is authored once as a _snippet_ in the shared cloud-step
209	home `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/` and stitched into each step at host-assembly time. The
210	home deliberately breaks the `rbgj«family»` letter scheme — a shared snippet
211	belongs to no single made-side family, so `s` is the no-family family.
212	
213	*Mechanism.* A step marks an inclusion point with a directive line
214	
215	----
216	#@rbgjs_include «name»
217	----
218	
219	(any leading indentation tolerated). At assembly, `zrbfc_expand_includes`
220	(`rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`, sibling to `zrbfc_write_script_body`, sourced by both the
221	Lode spine and the made-side `rbfca_` vouch assembler) rewrites the step body,
222	splicing in `rbgjs-«name».sh` with its leading shebang stripped. A body with no
223	marker is rewritten unchanged, so an assembler may call the expander for every
224	step. An unknown `«name»` fails the expander, which fails dispatch before submit
225	(crash-fast — no silent skip). The snippet shebang exists only so shellcheck
226	lints the fragment as bash; it never reaches the build.
227	
228	*Register channel — shell vars, not substitutions.* A snippet reads the plain
229	upper-case shell vars the kind sets immediately before the marker, and writes
230	plain shell vars the kind reads after — it is blind to `_RBGx_` substitution
231	names. This is load-bearing: it is precisely what lets one snippet serve callers
232	whose substitution namespaces are disjoint (e.g. Lode's `_RBGL_*` and
233	hallmark-verify's `_RBGV_*`). The substitution-register channel (automapped
234	`_RBGx_` keys) and the snippet shell-var channel are distinct; a snippet never
235	crosses into the substitution channel.
236	
237	*The library (each snippet's requires/provides):*
238	
239	[cols="1,3",options="header"]
240	|===
241	|Snippet |Contract
242	
243	|`token-fetch`        |_requires_ none (ambient metadata server); _provides_ `TOKEN`. The canonical skopeo-to-GAR metadata-server token (RBSCB), in-memory, never to `/workspace`.
244	|`skopeo-fingerprint` |_requires_ `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE`; _provides_ `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT`. Inspect the raw manifest, take its canonical sha256, derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
245	|`buildx-bootstrap`   |_requires_ none; _provides_ the `rb-builder` buildx builder, created-if-absent and selected. Idempotent under retry; run once per step.
246	|`buildx-push`        |_requires_ `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` (and the bootstrap builder); _provides_ the image pushed to `PUSH_URI`. The irreducible FROM-scratch push.
247	|===
248	
249	*Share the primitive, not the whole step.* The vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02`,
250	hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`) is NOT one shared step: the two diverge on five
251	load-bearing axes — push cardinality (loop-N members vs single), destination-URI
252	template and arity, platform source (literal `linux/amd64` vs a `/workspace`
253	platforms file), a conditional Dockerfile (conjure verify files), and disjoint
254	substitution sets. Only the buildx bootstrap and the push invocation are
255	identical, so only those are shared — as `buildx-bootstrap` + `buildx-push`.
256	Cardinality, URI construction, platform choice, and context assembly (which JSON,
257	which Dockerfile) stay in the per-kind step. The same boundary governs
258	`skopeo-fingerprint`: the inspect→digest→fingerprint core is identical across
259	captures, but the first GAR copy is excluded because its destination tag is
260	itself digest-derived and kind-specific, so it cannot be a clean shared
261	parameter. The rule the library follows: share the irreducible identical core,
262	leave kind-specific assembly in the kind.
263	
264	*In-heat reach (partial dedup, forward payoff).* `buildx-bootstrap`/`buildx-push`
265	have two live callers (`rbgjl02`, `rbgjv03`). `token-fetch` and
266	`skopeo-fingerprint` have one live caller each (bole's `rbgjl01`) this heat — the
267	other copy lives in enshrine (`rbgje01`), which is reference-not-migrated, so its
268	fork stays until that cutover. The snippets are authored once now regardless: the
269	library teaches "compose from the library," and the dedup completes when the
270	doomed forks are removed.
271

==== Decision

Cloud Build configuration files for trigger-based builds use `cloudbuild.json` format,
generated programmatically via `jq`.
YAML is not used.

==== Rationale

JSON is the Cloud Build REST API's native wire format.
The `projects.builds.create` and `projects.triggers.run` endpoints accept JSON request bodies
modeled on the API's `Build` resource.
YAML is a file-authoring convenience that gets parsed into the same `Build` structure before submission.

Programmatic generation via `jq` produces correct JSON directly — no YAML indentation
fragility, no multiline string quoting hazards, no dependency on `yq` or custom emitters.
The stitch function (`zrbf_stitch_build_json`) already generates JSON;
converting to YAML would add complexity with zero functional benefit.

==== Evidence

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Claim
|Source

|REST API is natively JSON
|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.builds/create[projects.builds.create API reference] — request body is a `Build` resource in JSON

|YAML and JSON are equivalent serializations
|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/build-config-file-schema[Build configuration file schema] — "You can write the build config file using the YAML or the JSON syntax"

|Triggers accept both formats
|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/automating-builds/create-manage-triggers[Create and manage build triggers] — "Cloud Build configuration file (yaml or json)"

|Trigger autodetect precedence includes JSON
|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/api/reference/rest/v1/projects.triggers[projects.triggers REST resource] — precedence: `cloudbuild.yaml`, `cloudbuild.yml`, `cloudbuild.json`, `Dockerfile`

|No deprecation signals through Feb 2026
|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/release-notes[Cloud Build release notes] — no mention of reducing JSON support

|SLSA provenance does not differ by format
|https://docs.google.com/build/docs/securing-builds/generate-validate-build-provenance[Generate and validate build provenance] — provenance records `buildConfigSource.path` (file name), not format-specific fields
|===

==== What Dies

* Any assumption that `cloudbuild.yaml` is required for triggers or SLSA provenance
* The linked term `rbtgr_build_yaml` — replaced by
{rbtgr_build_json}
* YAML-specific concerns: multiline string quoting, indentation sensitivity, `$$` escaping conventions

==== What Survives

* `zrbf_stitch_build_json()` — continues to emit JSON via `jq`, adapted for trigger structure
* `rbgjb/*.sh` step scripts — inlined into JSON `args` arrays at inscribe time
* All `_RBGY_*` substitution variables — baked into JSON `substitutions` object
* OCI Layout Bridge pattern — unchanged

==== Provenance Note

SLSA v1.0 provenance records `buildConfigSource.path` which will contain `cloudbuild.json`.
This is a cosmetic difference from `cloudbuild.yaml` — the structured provenance fields
(build inputs, parameters, outputs) are identical regardless of config file format.

==== References

* Research conducted: 2026-02-24 (web search + API documentation review)
* Google docs consistently present YAML examples by convention, not by requirement
* The `gcloud builds submit` CLI internally converts any config format to JSON for API submission

==== Capture Composition Contract (rblds_ spine)

This section governs the host-composed `projects.builds.create` path for Lode
captures — distinct from the trigger-autodetect path the Decision above covers.
A Lode capture is composed on the host by the capture-assembly spine
(`rblds_Spine.sh`) from two data inputs handed by a per-kind body (e.g. bole's
`zrbld_ensconce_submit`): an ordered _recipe_ of step rows and an opaque
_substitutions_ blob. The spine carries no per-kind branch and no
kind-registration mechanism — a new Lode kind is a new body file the spine
consumes as data, never a conditional inside the spine.

===== Spine/body boundary

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Owner |Carries

|Spine (`rblds_`)
|The step-composition loop (recipe rows -> `steps[]`); the Build-resource
envelope shape (`serviceAccount`, `options.automapSubstitutions`,
`options.logging`, `options.pool`, `timeout`); submit + build-id persist +
console link; the poll via `zrbfc_wait_build_completion`; and the
`buildStepOutputs[N]` base64-decode. The capture-domain constants — the mason
capture SA, the `TETHER` pool, the regime build timeout — are spine-owned,
because every Lode capture fetches upstream bytes as mason over the network.

|Body (`rbld«kind»_`)
|The recipe rows (pre-resolved); the substitutions blob; the poll ceiling (the
one kind-flavored build knob); and the interpretation of the decoded step
output (the per-member envelope fan-out).
|===

The only kind-flavored build knob the body passes is the poll ceiling (ensconce
borrows the enshrine ceiling). `serviceAccount`/`pool`/`timeout` are NOT body
parameters: they are capture-domain invariants the spine owns. A future kind
that needed a different pool or SA would change the spine's capture-domain
constants deliberately, not slip past as recipe data.

===== Recipe row format

A recipe is an ordered list of `|`-delimited step rows. The `|` delimiter is
load-bearing: builder image refs carry colons (tags and `sha256:` digests), so a
colon delimiter would be ambiguous.

----
script_path | builder_image | id | entrypoint
----

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Field |Meaning

|`script_path`   |Absolute path to the step script, _pre-resolved by the body_. The spine owns no steps-directory knowledge.
|`builder_image` |The step's builder image ref (a reliquary tool image).
|`id`            |The Cloud Build step id.
|`entrypoint`    |`bash` \| `sh` \| `python3` — selects the composed shebang line. Any other value fails composition.
|===

The spine reads each script's body (shebang stripped), expands any
`#@rbgjs_include` markers against the shared snippet library (see _Composed-snippet
library_ below), prepends the shebang the entrypoint selects, and inlines the
result as the step's `script` field. This is the same shape the made-side
`rbfca_` assemblers use; the capture spine is the canonical single copy for the
fetched side.

===== Substitutions blob — opaque

The body composes the substitutions object (the `_RBGL_*` keys) into a file; the
spine slots it verbatim into the Build resource's `substitutions` field and
reads no key from it. `options.automapSubstitutions` maps the keys into each
step's environment. The spine's opacity to the blob is what keeps it
registration-agnostic — a new kind authors a different blob without touching the
spine.

Invariant (RBSCB): substitutions and `/workspace` carry non-secret data only;
secrets are step-local and re-minted.

===== Extract contract

The spine's extract decodes one step's `buildStepOutputs[N]` slot — base64 to a
destination file — where `N` is the only generic parameter. The decoded JSON's
shape (for bole: `slot_1`..`slot_N` member envelopes, each a stamp + vouch) is
the body's knowledge; the spine is member-count-agnostic. This keeps the
N-member envelope axis out of the spine.

===== Substitution-coverage check (enforced by zrbld_spine_validate)

A composed step reads two register channels:

* _Substitution registers_ — `_RBGL_*` keys present in the substitutions blob,
  automapped into every step's environment.
* _/workspace registers_ — non-secret files a step writes for a later step,
  named by path relative to `/workspace`.

The two channels are validated differently, because they differ in what can be
recovered safely from the step source. The substitution channel — where an
undefined reference is both silent (it expands to empty in-build) and expensive
to surface (it costs the whole cook) — is checked by derivation at dispatch; the
`/workspace` channel is left to the steps' own in-build guards.

*Substitution channel — derived, not declared.* A step's substitution _requires_
is exactly the set of `_RBGL_*` names its body references — the references ARE the
requires, so nothing separate is declared and nothing can drift from the code. At
dispatch `zrbld_spine_validate` scans each step's include-expanded body for
`_RBGL_*` tokens on non-comment lines and fails before submit at the first token
absent from the substitutions blob's keys, naming the offending register. Coverage
is _flat_: substitutions automap into every step, so a register is satisfied by
its presence in the blob alone — there is no recipe order and no cross-step
`provides`. The scan runs on the _include-expanded_ body; composed snippets carry
no `_RBGL_*` references (they speak the shell-var channel — see below), so only the
kind's own substitution reads are recovered.

*Workspace channel — guarded in-step, dispatch-validation deferred.* A step's
`/workspace` reads and writes are not safely recoverable by a static scan: the
paths are templated by runtime loop variables (e.g. `lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json`)
and read-vs-write classification in shell (redirections, `test -f`, `cp` of a
var-held path) is exactly what a scanner gets wrong. The live recipes already
guard their own `/workspace` preconditions at runtime — the vouch-push step opens
with `test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt || FATAL "step 01 must run first"` — so
the channel is crash-fast in-build. A dispatch-time `/workspace` declaration is
added only when a future kind needs one, not authored speculatively (generalize
against the instances we have, never stub the ones we don't).

The derivation is shaped by the capture domain's three axes:

* _N-member envelopes_ — the terminal step's single `buildStepOutputs` slot is
  body knowledge (member fan-out lives in the body), so it never enters the
  coverage check.
* _Tool variety_ — a `_RBGL_*` name appears as the same literal token whether a
  step is bash (`${_RBGL_X}`) or `python3` (`os.environ['_RBGL_X']`), so the scan
  is identical across builder image and entrypoint.
* _Opaque-blob payloads_ — the check tests key _presence_ by NAME, never value
  shape, preserving the spine's opacity to the blob.

===== Composed-snippet library (rbgjs)

Cloud Build offers no native step reuse. Where the same in-step logic recurs
across cloud steps, it is authored once as a _snippet_ in the shared cloud-step
home `Tools/rbk/rbgjs/` and stitched into each step at host-assembly time. The
home deliberately breaks the `rbgj«family»` letter scheme — a shared snippet
belongs to no single made-side family, so `s` is the no-family family.

*Mechanism.* A step marks an inclusion point with a directive line

----
#@rbgjs_include «name»
----

(any leading indentation tolerated). At assembly, `zrbfc_expand_includes`
(`rbfcb_BuildHost.sh`, sibling to `zrbfc_write_script_body`, sourced by both the
Lode spine and the made-side `rbfca_` vouch assembler) rewrites the step body,
splicing in `rbgjs-«name».sh` with its leading shebang stripped. A body with no
marker is rewritten unchanged, so an assembler may call the expander for every
step. An unknown `«name»` fails the expander, which fails dispatch before submit
(crash-fast — no silent skip). The snippet shebang exists only so shellcheck
lints the fragment as bash; it never reaches the build.

*Register channel — shell vars, not substitutions.* A snippet reads the plain
upper-case shell vars the kind sets immediately before the marker, and writes
plain shell vars the kind reads after — it is blind to `_RBGx_` substitution
names. This is load-bearing: it is precisely what lets one snippet serve callers
whose substitution namespaces are disjoint (e.g. Lode's `_RBGL_*` and
hallmark-verify's `_RBGV_*`). The substitution-register channel (automapped
`_RBGx_` keys) and the snippet shell-var channel are distinct; a snippet never
crosses into the substitution channel.

*The library (each snippet's requires/provides):*

[cols="1,3",options="header"]
|===
|Snippet |Contract

|`token-fetch`        |_requires_ none (ambient metadata server); _provides_ `TOKEN`. The canonical skopeo-to-GAR metadata-server token (RBSCB), in-memory, never to `/workspace`.
|`skopeo-fingerprint` |_requires_ `ORIGIN`, `RAW_FILE`; _provides_ `SHA`, `FINGERPRINT`. Inspect the raw manifest, take its canonical sha256, derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
|`buildx-bootstrap`   |_requires_ none; _provides_ the `rb-builder` buildx builder, created-if-absent and selected. Idempotent under retry; run once per step.
|`buildx-push`        |_requires_ `PUSH_URI`, `PUSH_PLATFORMS`, `PUSH_CTX` (and the bootstrap builder); _provides_ the image pushed to `PUSH_URI`. The irreducible FROM-scratch push.
|===

*Share the primitive, not the whole step.* The vouch-push step (Lode `rbgjl02`,
hallmark-verify `rbgjv03`) is NOT one shared step: the two diverge on five
load-bearing axes — push cardinality (loop-N members vs single), destination-URI
template and arity, platform source (literal `linux/amd64` vs a `/workspace`
platforms file), a conditional Dockerfile (conjure verify files), and disjoint
substitution sets. Only the buildx bootstrap and the push invocation are
identical, so only those are shared — as `buildx-bootstrap` + `buildx-push`.
Cardinality, URI construction, platform choice, and context assembly (which JSON,
which Dockerfile) stay in the per-kind step. The same boundary governs
`skopeo-fingerprint`: the inspect→digest→fingerprint core is identical across
captures, but the first GAR copy is excluded because its destination tag is
itself digest-derived and kind-specific, so it cannot be a clean shared
parameter. The rule the library follows: share the irreducible identical core,
leave kind-specific assembly in the kind.

*In-heat reach (partial dedup, forward payoff).* `buildx-bootstrap`/`buildx-push`
have two live callers (`rbgjl02`, `rbgjv03`). `token-fetch` and
`skopeo-fingerprint` have one live caller each (bole's `rbgjl01`) this heat — the
other copy lives in enshrine (`rbgje01`), which is reference-not-migrated, so its
fork stays until that cutover. The snippets are authored once now regardless: the
library teaches "compose from the library," and the dedup completes when the
doomed forks are removed.

Mounted. Here's where ₢BXAAG actually stands — and it's not where the docket implies.

## ₣BX · cloud-build-spec-record (₢BXAAG)

**Goal (one sentence):** record the heat's rationale in the Cloud Build specs — the credential/duplication posture in RBSCB, and the spine/composition/contract decision as an RBSCJ section.

**Heat context:** `rbk-09-capture-machinery-unification` — a behavior-preserving refactor unifying the fetched-side capture machinery (host spine + shared cloud step-library) with one declared behavior-adding sliver (the recipe validator). 8/12 done; four remain: this one, the skirmish gate (₢BXAAH), the CBG capstone (₢BXAAI), and a freshly-slated workspace-quoin pace (₢BXAAM). The pace's own Character is *"recording cinched rationale alongside the code."*

## The honest finding

This pace appears to have been satisfied incrementally by the paces that preceded it. "Alongside the code" turned out to be literal — the rationale got recorded *in* paces D/E/F, not deferred to here:

## Confirm: the heat's recorded rationale is coherent and complete

**Every cross-reference the new sections assert is live and correctly located.** All ten function names the recorded text names resolve to real definitions, and — notably — the ones that moved in this heat's own decompositions landed exactly where the relocation discipline promised, prefix intact:

The one `requires/provides` hit in RBSCJ is the *snippet library's* shell-var contract (token-fetch provides `TOKEN`, etc.) — a different, current channel that the substitution-coverage subsection explicitly distinguishes.
